hints.oriens: 'Oriens' is a participle/noun meaning 'the rising one / the dawn'
hints.alto: 'Alto' is a noun/adjective meaning 'the height / on high'

hints.present indicative: Present would be 'visitat'; 'visitavit' with -avit is perfect

hints.video, videre, vidi, visum: These are the principal parts of 'video' (to see)
hints.viso, visere, visi, visum: These are the principal parts of 'viso' (to go to see)
hints.inviso, invisere, invisi, invisum: These are the principal parts of 'inviso' (to look at)

hints.nominative: Nominative 'nos' means 'we'; here it is the direct object of 'visitavit'

steps.reveal: oriens = the dawn / the rising one (nom.)

hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'orientem'; 'oriens' is nominative, the subject

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns end in -a; 'oriens' has genitive 'orientis'
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um; 'oriens' has genitive 'orientis'
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have -us genitive; 'oriens' has genitive 'orientis'

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'altum' or 'altus'; 'alto' with -o is ablative after 'ex'
hints.dative: Dative would also be 'alto', but after 'ex' it is ablative of source

translation: The dawn from on high has visited us
